CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). It also added scenario test coverage (`FleetCRUD.cs`) exercising CRUD operations for ManagedNamespace. The unreleased `1.2.0-beta.2` CHANGELOG section was not updated.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG not updated**: The `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section under `### Features Added` is empty despite ManagedNamespace support being added\n- **README Examples section is a stub**: The `## Examples` section only links to generic SDK samples; no concrete code examples or snippets exist for any Fleet resource types\n- **ManagedNamespace not mentioned in README**: The new `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` type, its CRUD operations, and key concepts (DefaultResourceQuota, DefaultNetworkPolicy, etc.) are completely absent from the README\n- **No Samples test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed samples; the `## Examples` section cannot link to testable code examples\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md`\n\nEdit the `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section to add the ManagedNamespace entry:\n\n```markdown\n## 1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added `ManagedNamespace` resource support (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`), enabling creation, update, deletion, and enumeration of managed namespaces within a Fleet.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n1. **Create** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\n\n   Provide at minimum two snippet-backed samples:\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet` – authenticate, create a Fleet resource\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace` – create a ManagedNamespace under an existing Fleet\n\n   Example structure:\n\n   ```csharp\n   // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n   // Licensed under the MIT License.\n\n   using System;\n   using System.Threading.Tasks;\n   using Azure.Core.TestFramework;\n   using Azure.Identity;\n   using Azure.ResourceManager;\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet;\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models;\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\n   using NUnit.Framework;\n\n   namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples\n   {\n       public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\u003cContainerServiceFleetManagementTestEnvironment\u003e\n       {\n           [Test]\n           [SyncOnly]\n           [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n           public async Task CreateFleetAndManagedNamespace()\n           {\n               #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet\n               ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n               SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n               ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\"\u003cresource-group\u003e\");\n               ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\n               ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\"eastus\"));\n               ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetResource\u003e createOp = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"\u003cfleet-name\u003e\", fleetData);\n               ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = createOp.Value;\n               #endregion\n\n               #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection namespaces = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces();\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\"eastus\"));\n               ArmOperation\u003cContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource\u003e nsOp = await namespaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"\u003cnamespace-name\u003e\", nsData);\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource ns = nsOp.Value;\n               Console.WriteLine($\"Created namespace: {ns.Data.Name}\");\n               #endregion\n           }\n       }\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Update README** at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`:\n\n   Replace the current `## Examples` section content with snippet placeholders:\n\n   ```markdown\n   ## Examples\n\n   ### Create a Fleet\n\n   ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet\n   ```\n\n   ### Create a ManagedNamespace\n\n   ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace\n   ```\n   ```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` currently has all required sections but needs the following improvements:\n\n| Section | Status | Action needed |\n|---|---|---|\n| Getting started \u003e Install the package | ✅ Present | None |\n| Getting started \u003e Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None |\n| Getting started \u003e Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add bullet points describing Fleet, FleetMember, ManagedNamespace, UpdateRun, Gate resource types |\n| Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders as described above |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | None |\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | None |\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | None |\n\nFor the **Key concepts** section, add a description along the lines of:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n- **Fleet** (`ContainerServiceFleetResource`): Top-level resource representing a group of AKS clusters managed together.\n- **FleetMember** (`ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource`): An AKS cluster enrolled in the Fleet.\n- **ManagedNamespace** (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`): A namespace propagated and managed across all Fleet member clusters, with configurable resource quotas and network policies.\n- **UpdateRun** (`ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource`): A sequenced rollout of Kubernetes version upgrades across Fleet members.\n- **Gate** (`ContainerServiceFleetGateResource`): A signal-based gate that can pause an UpdateRun until a condition is satisfied.\n```\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 fleet` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 fleet` — if any public types changed\n5. `dotnet format sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to 2026-04-07T02:27:24.6146880Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6146955Z ## What Changed
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6146960Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6148155Z PR #57267 added `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, and related models/data types representing the new `ManagedNamespace` resource under a Fleet. It also added scenario test coverage (`FleetCRUD.cs`) exercising CRUD operations for ManagedNamespace. The unreleased `1.2.0-beta.2` CHANGELOG section was not updated.
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6148160Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6148233Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6148237Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6148633Z - **CHANGELOG not updated**: The `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section under `### Features Added` is empty despite ManagedNamespace support being added
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6149092Z - **README Examples section is a stub**: The `## Examples` section only links to generic SDK samples; no concrete code examples or snippets exist for any Fleet resource types
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6149812Z - **ManagedNamespace not mentioned in README**: The new `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` type, its CRUD operations, and key concepts (DefaultResourceQuota, DefaultNetworkPolicy, etc.) are completely absent from the README
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150232Z - **No Samples test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed samples; the `## Examples` section cannot link to testable code examples
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150243Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150312Z <details>
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150509Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150522Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150810Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150815Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150895Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6150905Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151101Z #### `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md`
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151162Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151349Z Edit the `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section to add the ManagedNamespace entry:
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151359Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151481Z ```markdown
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151562Z ## 1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151567Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151651Z ### Features Added
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6151655Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6152566Z - Added `ManagedNamespace` resource support (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`), enabling creation, update, deletion, and enumeration of managed namespaces within a Fleet.
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6152637Z ```
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6152640Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6152731Z ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6152741Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153062Z 1. **Create** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153067Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153184Z    Provide at minimum two snippet-backed samples:
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153482Z    - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet` – authenticate, create a Fleet resource
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153860Z    - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace` – create a ManagedNamespace under an existing Fleet
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153871Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153947Z    Example structure:
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6153952Z 
2026-04-07T02:27:24.6154018Z    ```csharp
